body.u: I am sitting in front of the television, feeling rather fed up with life, when I hear voices and laughter outside. I cross over to the window and look out. There are a group of teenagers on their way home from school, about five girls and five boys. I wonder if they are in their last year at school, just about to take their exams.
They laugh and joke, crossing the road without looking properly and they join their friends in the crowded coffee bar.

I remember back to when my life followed the same daily routine as theirs. My last year at school having great times, then taking my exams, and gradually saying goodbye to most of my friends as they left school.
Like them I left and went to college for two years, to do social welfare. There another crowd of new friends. Saying goodbye to them, as gradually we drifted apart and got jobs.

I still lived at home and life carried on much the same. But there was one difference I was working as a social worker. I suddenly felt a lot older and although I had left school just two years ago it seemed a lifetime ago.
I got another job and moved off of the island and moved into a flat with a few other girls.

I loved my job and the city life, I had a busy social life and at one party my whole life seemed to change. I met Dave and we got on really well straight away. After a year or so, we were engaged. I didn’t want to get married early, so we waited three years and then married. We had bought a small house near London, and were very happy I still had my career but after a year gave it up to start a family. We had three children two boys, then a girl. As they grew older and went to school, I went back to my career.
Eventually the children left home, and led their own lifeslives.
Dave and I were about forty-five by this time. We saved and with the children gone, had a lovely time travelling together.
Of course we were getting older and we both retired. We have a lazy life now which can get very boring at times.

The teenagers are leaving the coffee bar now, probably going home, doing homework, then they will be off out somewhere to have some fun. As I watch them disappear round the corner, I cannot help but envy them. What I would give to be in my ‘teens’ again.
